steps.title: Move everything to one side and factor
steps.content: Start with $x - a = (x-a)(x-24)$. Subtract the right side: $(x-a) - (x-a)(x-24) = 0$. Factor out $(x-a)$: $(x-a)\bigl[1 - (x-24)\bigr] = 0$, which simplifies to $(x-a)(25-x) = 0$.

steps.title: Read off the solutions
steps.content: By the zero-product property, $x - a = 0$ or $25 - x = 0$, so $x = a$ or $x = 25$. Because $a > 25$, these are two distinct solutions and $x = 24$ is not one of them.

steps.title: Test the statements
steps.content:
• Statement I — $x = a$: This is one of the two roots of $(x-a)(25-x) = 0$. True.
  • Statement II — $x = 24$: Substitute into the original: LHS $= 24 - a$, RHS $= (24 - a)(24 - 24) = 0$. They are equal only when $24 - a = 0$, i.e. $a = 24$. But $a > 25$, so LHS $\ne 0$ and the equation fails. False.
  • Statement III — $x = 25$: Substitute: LHS $= 25 - a$, RHS $= (25 - a)(25 - 24) = 25 - a$. Equal for every $a$, so this is always a solution. True.
So I and III only. That matches choice B — B is correct.
